menu.title: Menu
menu.cookies_text: This website uses only technical cookies necessary for the proper functioning of the application. We do not use any tracking or marketing cookies. Your preferences are stored locally in your browser.
menu.gdpr_text: We process your personal data only to the extent necessary to provide the service. If you are logged in, we store your recipes and favorite products. You have the right to access, correct or delete your data. Contact us for more information.
menu.contact_subject_placeholder: What would you like to write about?

home.title: LiquiMixer
home.subtitle: E-Liquid Mixing Calculator
home.description: Calculate precise PG/VG ratios, nicotine and flavor percentages for your custom e-liquids
home.warning_text: When mixing e-liquid, always wear protective gloves and work in a well-ventilated area, especially when using nicotine boosters. Use only high-quality ingredients without additives and with food-grade certification suitable for vaping from verified suppliers. Follow precise ingredient ratios. Store the finished liquid out of reach of children and pets.
prep.title: Nicotine Base Preparation
prep.description: First prepare your nicotine base by diluting the nicotine booster
prep.pg_detail: Propylene glycol is a colorless, odorless liquid that forms the base of e-liquids. It carries flavor and creates the characteristic "throat hit" sensation. It is hygroscopic, so store it in a sealed container. PG is generally considered safe for inhalation and is also used in food and pharmaceutical industries.
prep.vg_detail: Vegetable glycerin is a thick, sweet liquid derived from vegetable oils. It is responsible for creating dense vapor and adds subtle sweetness to the liquid. The higher the VG ratio, the thicker the vapor but less pronounced flavor. VG is suitable for direct lung (DL) inhalation and high-powered vaping devices.
prep.flavors_detail: E-liquid flavors are concentrated aromas specially designed for vaping. Use only flavors intended for e-liquids, not food-grade aromas! Concentration typically ranges between 5-20% depending on the flavor type. Fruit flavors are more intense, dessert and tobacco flavors require higher percentages and longer steeping.
prep.nicotine_detail: Nicotine Booster: Traditional form of nicotine with a more pronounced throat hit. Suitable for regular vaping. Nicotine Salt: Smoother form of nicotine that absorbs faster and has a milder throat hit even at higher concentrations. Suitable for pod systems and MTL devices. WARNING: Nicotine is poison! Always wear gloves and work in a well-ventilated area.
prep.gloves_detail: Nitrile or latex gloves are essential when working with nicotine. Nicotine is easily absorbed through the skin and can cause poisoning. Gloves also protect against contamination of the mixture and maintain sterility. Use disposable gloves and dispose of them safely after use. If nicotine contacts skin, wash thoroughly with soap and water.
prep.bottle_detail: Glass bottles are ideal for storing e-liquids as they don't react with the contents and don't affect taste. Dark glass (amber) protects from UV radiation and extends shelf life. Bottles with droppers or narrow necks are suitable for mixing. Always clean and dry bottles thoroughly before use.
prep.dropper_detail: Measuring tools are essential for precise dosing of ingredients. Dropper: Suitable for smaller volumes, less precise. Pipette: More precise than dropper, suitable for medium volumes. Syringe (without needle): Most precise option, ideal for nicotine. Use separate tools for each ingredient to avoid contamination.
prep.measuring_detail: For precise mixing you need reliable measuring tools. Graduated cylinders: For measuring volume in ml. Measuring syringes: Different sizes for different ingredients (1ml for nicotine, 5-10ml for VG/PG). Always measure twice and mix once!
